frameworks.name: linear gated network
frameworks.one_line: jointly optimized weights and gates with neuron-like fast bounded nonnegative gates

frameworks.name: gradient descent learning dynamics
frameworks.one_line: analytic reduction of joint weight-gate optimization to an effective eigenspace

frameworks.name: modular task abstraction
frameworks.one_line: weights self-organize into task/sub-task modules switched by gating representations

frameworks.name: cognitive flexibility / task switching
frameworks.one_line: neuroscience phenomenon of selecting appropriate task abstractions modeled mechanistically

core_question: How can flexible task abstractions emerge in neural systems via joint optimization of weights and biologically constrained gates?
key_assumptions: gates operate on a faster timescale than weights
gate activity is nonnegative and bounded
weights and gates are jointly optimized by gradient descent
learning dynamics admit an effective low-dimensional eigenspace
manipulations: imposing fast, nonnegative, bounded constraints on gates
varying curriculum block size and amount of task training
extending from linear to nonlinear network across two tasks
key_findings: weights self-organize into modules specialized for tasks or sub-tasks
gates form unique representations that switch the appropriate weight modules
task switching in the gating layer accelerates with curriculum block size and training
discovered abstractions support generalization via task and subtask composition
interpretations: a virtuous cycle: fast gates protect prior knowledge driving weight specialization, which in turn speeds gate updates
emergence of modular task abstractions mirrors key cognitive-neuroscience findings on task switching
